channel.title: Naharaim
channel.description: A RSS feed containing the latest articles published in Naharaim

item.title: Introduction
item.description: Article by Dani Kranz DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/naha-2024-0025

item.title: “God Who Brought us Close and Then Repented”: Hester Panim and Revelation in Avraham Ben-Yitzhak’s Writings
item.description: Article by Dani Kranz DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/naha-2023-0016

item.title: Hebrew Dreams in the Berlin of Yesterday: German-Jewish Symbiosis Fantasy on the City’s Streets in Lifney Hamakom by Haim Be’er and Avedot by Lea Goldberg
item.description: Article by Dani Kranz DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/naha-2023-0004

item.title: Understanding the Meaning of “Aspeset”: Hermeneutical Approaches, Reading Practices and the Departure from Germany in Two Lea Goldberg Novels
item.description: Article by Dani Kranz DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/naha-2023-0006

item.title: Hortulus 37, 1959: Translation as Collaboration in an Anthology of New Poetry from Israel
item.description: Article by Dani Kranz DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/naha-2023-0010

item.title: Ethical Implications of German-Hebrew Homophony: Analyzing Dan Pagis’ “Sealed Railcar” Cycle
item.description: Article by Dani Kranz DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/naha-2023-0007

item.title: Franz Rosenzweig’s and Paul Celan’s Early German Translations of Yehudah Halevi’s Hebrew Poems
item.description: Article by Dani Kranz DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/naha-2023-0002

item.title: Die Muttersprache, die schweigt, und die Stiefmuttersprache, die erzählt. Zu Aharon Appelfelds Sprachpoetik zwischen Deutsch und Hebräisch
item.description: Article by Dani Kranz DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/naha-2023-0014

item.title: On Translated Literature’s Intended Reader(s): The Case of Yoram Kaniuk’s The Last Berliner
item.description: Article by Dani Kranz DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/naha-2023-0021

item.title: The Grammar of Displacement: Entwined Stories in Ruth Almog and Jenny Erpenbeck
item.description: Article by Dani Kranz DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/naha-2023-0008

item.title: Exploring Israel/Palestine Through the Eyes of Writers: German-Language Authors and Undiscriminating Anthropological Glasses
item.description: Article by Dani Kranz DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/naha-2023-0009
